method: VADER sentiment + curated keyword-density (per 1k words), reusing nyt_trans_analysis.py FRAMING_CATEGORIES lexicons. Deterministic; no model calls.
notes: Headline corroboration: conflict-vocabulary density correlates r=0.41 with the models' conflict-framing scores across 3200 articles. Framing *direction* is only weakly tracked by any keyword/sentiment baseline (|r|~0.13) — subtle directional framing is exactly what a bag-of-words method cannot read, which is why models were used for it. The disagreement queue ranks by standardized VADER-vs-model direction divergence.
